rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical nitroindolylation bifunctionalization of arylalkenes using NaNO2 as nitro radical source, LiBr as mediator, and indoles to install -NO2 and indolyl groups across styrenes
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of nitrite (NO2-) to nitro free radical (NO2•), facilitated by Cu(I)/Cu(II) and B(C6F5)3; bromide is also oxidized to Br• which traps the benzylic radical; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbon radical Int I from NO2 addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation Int II from Cu(II) oxidation of Int I
nitrostyrene side product (path d, H elimination)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes NO2- to •NO2 (mediated by Cu(I)→Cu(II) and B(C6F5)3); •NO2 adds to styrene 1a giving benzylic radical Int I, which Cu(II) further oxidizes to benzylic cation Int II; Br- captures Int II to form bromonitro intermediate 5, which undergoes SN2 by N-methylindole at C3 to deliver 4aa, while the cathode reduces H+ to H2.
rate_determining_step: SN2 displacement of bromide in intermediate 5 by C3 of N-methylindole to form the C-C bond

probe_reagents.name: step-by-step loading (1a + NaNO2 first, then indole 3a)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 4aa obtained in 82% yield, supporting stepwise formation of a bromonitro intermediate before indole addition

probe_reagents.name: independently prepared bromonitro compound 5
probe_reagents.observation: 5 + indole 3a with B(C6F5)3 gave 4aa in 97% yield, confirming 5 as on-cycle intermediate; structure verified by 1H/13C NMR

probe_reagents.name: NaNO3 additive
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows strong oxidation peak at 0.62 V for NaNO2+NaNO3 vs 0.64 V for NaNO2 alone, indicating nitrate enhances Faradaic efficiency of nitrite oxidation

probe_reagents.name: B(C6F5)3
probe_reagents.observation: Lewis acid promotes nitrite oxidation to •NO2 (synergistic effect seen in CV); 0.5 equiv used in standard conditions

notes: EIS (impedance spectroscopy) data also support the role of NaNO3 in lowering charge-transfer resistance. Two competing side pathways are noted: path b giving 3-nitroindole (direct nitration of indole) and path d giving β-nitrostyrene via H-elimination from Int II; these must be suppressed. No TEMPO/BHT radical trap or KIE experiments were reported; mechanism is supported mainly by CV/EIS, stepwise loading, and isolation of bromonitro intermediate 5.
